data.headline: 5 Reasons Dogs With Gunky, Itchy Ears Are Switching to Good For Pets
data.subheadline: If you've cleaned, dropped and treated the same ear over and over, only for it to come back smelly and gunky weeks later, you're not failing your dog. You've just been treating the wrong end. Here's what thousands of UK owners worked out, and why one small daily capsule changed things.
data.trustLine: 20,000+ dogs helped in the last 12 months · Backed by 4,537+ verified reviews · 90-day money-back guarantee

data.headline: You've tried everything for those ears. We know.
data.intro: If you're reading this, you've probably already been round the houses. Drops. Cleaners. Wipes. Vinegar mixes off the internet. Trips to the vet that cost a small fortune and bought you a few quiet weeks before the gunk came straight back. It's exhausting, and it's the kind of thing that makes a good owner feel like they're letting their dog down.

painPoints.title: The ears that never stay clean
painPoints.body: You clean them out, they look better for a day or two, then the smell and the gunk return. Recurring, no matter how diligent you are.

painPoints.title: The constant head-shaking and scratching
painPoints.body: That scratch-flick-scratch you hear from across the room. A dog that can't settle because something on the inside is irritating them.

painPoints.title: The money disappearing at the vet
painPoints.body: Ear drops, steroids and repeat cleaning appointments that add up fast and only ever calm things for a while. As one owner put it, the vet was costing £140 every two weeks.

painPoints.title: The nagging feeling you're missing something
painPoints.body: You've done the right things. So why does it keep coming back? Because most of those things treat the symptom in the ear, never the cause underneath.

data.headline: Co-developed with a practising UK vet

stats.label: dogs helped in the last 12 months

reviews.quote: Daily probiotics and enzymes make a meaningful difference to a dog's digestion.
reviews.author: Dr Kishan Vara MRCVS, practising UK vet and co-developer of the formula

data.heading: 1. A lot of owners see a difference within days
paragraphs: We won't promise you an overnight miracle, because the gut takes time to genuinely rebalance and we'd rather be straight with you. But the honest pattern in the reviews is that the head-shaking, the scratching and the gunk often start to ease early, sometimes in the first week or two.

Julie put it plainly: "I started these on the 24th January 2025 and today I actually saw a difference, no more paw licking or chewing. I cleaned her ears out today and nothing in there either. A much more relaxed and happy dog."

That early shift is usually the gut settling first. The deeper skin, coat and ear changes tend to build over the following weeks. Which is exactly why there's a 90-day money-back guarantee to cover the wait.

data.headline: It starts in the gut, not the ear
data.subheadline: Here's the reframe most owners have never been told: a lot of recurring ear trouble, itching and paw licking traces back to the gut, because the gut and skin are linked. Calm the inside and the outside has its best chance to follow.

steps.title: Flood the gut with good bacteria
steps.body: Five research-backed strains, Lactobacillus acidophilus, rhamnosus, brevis, plantarum and Bifidobacterium lactis, at 5 billion live cultures per serving. That's up to 20x the live cultures of some competitors.

steps.title: Feed them so they establish
steps.body: A chicory-root prebiotic (inulin) feeds the beneficial bacteria so they take hold and stay effective, rather than passing straight through.

steps.title: Rebalance, don't mask
steps.body: As the microbiome rebalances, it supports natural immune defences and a healthy yeast balance, the things that sit behind a lot of the ear and skin trouble owners chase from the outside.

steps.title: Get there alive
steps.body: It's cold-filled in a sealed capsule, heat and moisture free, to protect the live cultures that baked chews can degrade. A big number on a tub means nothing if the bacteria are already dead.

data.heading: 2. It works on every kind of ear trouble, because it works on the cause
paragraphs: Gunky, itchy, smelly, waxy, yeasty, red. Owners describe their dog's ears every which way, and the frustrating part is that creams and drops tend to be aimed at one symptom at a time.

Supporting the gut–skin connection and a healthy yeast balance works at the root instead of the surface, which is why the reviews cover such a range of ear stories. Tanya's words: "I had my boy on the baked alternative prior to seeing these and they slightly helped his ear problems. These are another level completely. What a difference they have made."
Same approach, whatever the ear looks like: settle the gut, support the immune and yeast balance, and give the ear a chance to stop flaring in the first place.

data.heading: 3. It's a gentle daily formula, suitable across breeds and sizes
paragraphs: So many of the owners who write in have rescues, or dogs with allergies and sensitive stomachs that react to everything. That's exactly the dog this was built to be safe and gentle for, with human-grade ingredients, no unhealthy fillers or grains, and a half-dose start to ease them in.

Katie adopted hers back in 2018 and had spent years on steroids, ear drops and cleaning: "Two and a half weeks on these and the difference is already massive. Her ears are now practically clean and there's no itching at all."

It's suitable for daily, long-term use across breeds and sizes (from 12 weeks of age, and not for pregnant or lactating dogs). The full ingredient list is printed on the pack, so you can check it against anything your dog reacts to.

data.heading: Just the good stuff. Nothing bulking it out.
paragraphs: We're not going to dress this up. Inside each capsule are five research-backed probiotic strains, a chicory-root prebiotic (inulin) to feed them, and a digestive enzyme complex to help break food down. That's it.
No unhealthy fillers. No grains. Human-grade ingredients, made in the UK. The kind of label you can actually read, rather than a mystery powder from an unnamed factory abroad.
We'd rather show you the label than scare you into buying. That's the whole point.

data.heading: 4. Giving it takes about ten seconds a day
paragraphs: No pilling. No fight. No standing over your dog with a cleaner while they pull away.
It's a sprinkle capsule: you twist it open and mix the contents straight into their food, once a day, dosed by weight. Even fussy dogs take it without a fuss.
After everything you've been through with this dog's ears, the easiest part of the day should be the bit that actually helps.

data.headline: Honest answers to the questions owners ask

items.q: How much do I give my dog?
items.a: It's dosed by weight: 1 capsule a day up to 25kg, 2 capsules for 25–40kg, and 3 capsules for 40kg and over. Twist the capsule open and mix it into food. Start on half the daily amount for the first 5–7 days, then move to the full dose.

items.q: How long until I see a difference with the ears?
items.a: It varies by dog. Some owners notice changes in the first couple of weeks, often firmer stools first. Ear, skin and coat changes usually build over 8–12 weeks as the microbiome genuinely rebalances rather than masking the symptom. The 90-day guarantee is there to cover that window.

items.q: What if it doesn't work for my dog?
items.a: Then you get your money back. There's a 90-day money-back guarantee, even on an opened tub, so the risk sits with us, not you.
It was co-developed with practising UK vet Dr Kishan Vara MRCVS. It's a daily supplement, not a prescription, and it works alongside your vet's advice rather than replacing it.

items.q: Can puppies and senior dogs take it?
items.a: It's suitable from 12 weeks of age, and seniors often benefit most from daily gut support. It's not suitable for puppies under 12 weeks or for pregnant or lactating dogs.

items.q: How fast is shipping, and what does it cost?
items.a: Orders ship on free 48-hour delivery, and on a subscription you can pause or cancel anytime.
51% of our profits go to UK animal welfare charities including the RSPCA, Dogs Trust and Guide Dogs. It's the model the business is built on, so when you help your dog, you help a dog who has no one.
